title: valeriana vulgaris var. stolonifera
intro: Valeriana vulgaris var. stolonifera, also known as creeping valerian, is a perennial plant species in the valerian family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include sedative and antispasmodic effects, and has been used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness. The plant's health benefits include promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, as well as acting as a natural muscle relaxant and pain reliever. Herbal preparations of creeping valerian include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and therapeutic benefits.
